article.title: Need Guidance 🙏
article.content:
**Hi i am A recent graduate, with no job and iNternship and i am from ece background from tier 3 college, in my college their is not that much coding culture, in the end of my final year i realized i waste all my 4 years now i regret it  , now i am jobless ,** 

**i don't know any tech stack and DSA and now i like to start the DSA but i have a something in mind that now i start the DSA or find any job and get any job along doing DSA or if i start the DSA what would be the time we master it and how much time it would intially takes , and if start the DSA that first i need to learn the all topics at first and then start solve the problems or learn one topic at a time and solve problem on my current topic. or would u refer any paid course or sheets Please ,**

 **cause i don't know what i am solving and what topic and what are the problems and i am currently doing DSA in java and what tech stack is good , literally i don't know a lot of thing , i don't get that how start a new topic and how many days to take to solve or master that topic and what would be the order to new topic and how , i confused every day and mind is this full of shit , please i need some guidence** .

**Thanks in Advance.**

post.content: If you have not done anything, and don\'t have any brand in your resume for example college or previous work experience, I think it will be hard to get even your resume screened with only DSA.\nWas in a similar situation, but my case was different. I had a good college but I changed my career after graduation. With DSA only I couldn\'t show any real work. So after some initial months with DSA, I dropped DSA and I started development, learning skills and making projects. The goal was to only set the foot in the industry. And I did that within a year from scratch. Without the need for DSA. And with my own capabilities, without any college tag. \nIf I was in your place, I would spend majority time in development and learning how to make real applications. Split would be like 70% development and 30% DSA, since you have all the time in the world now. If you are comfortable with Java then stick to it. After learning core Java, learn frameworks like Springboot, etc, whatever interests you. Also learn some CS fundamentals with DBMS being the most important. Showcase your work. Build key projects and put them on your resume and start reaching out to companies. That will require a lot of work, but it is the only way. You will get a job. Once you have set foot in the industry, work hard and prove your self. And then plan the trajectory where you want to go.
try gate and come better. \nIf financial better off, give GRE and leave India for MS in USA
relax nothing to worry about your first priority should be finding a job then you can do DSA along side
